text: "Honestly, the biggest thing for me is clarity and structure — I lean heavily on headers to break things up so engineers can scan and find what they need without reading everything top to bottom. I like to include code examples where they make sense, because sometimes seeing it is just faster than reading three paragraphs about it. The main thing I keep in mind is that my audience is competent — they're real engineers, not beginners — but they're probably new to whatever specific system I'm documenting, so I try to give enough context without being condescending about the fundamentals."
